sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content: The Beneficiary has held leading roles in distinguished organizations within the field of communication and market research, most notably at Peking University. Related evidence can be found in Exhibit X.

sections.heading: 1. Distinguished Reputation of Peking University
sections.content:
Peking University ("PKU") is among the most distinguished academic institutions in the world, having been "founded in 1898 as a replacement for the ancient Guozijian school (Imperial College)." [Exhibit X - x] As the "oldest higher education institution in China," PKU played a pivotal role in the nation's intellectual history, serving as "a center for Chinese progressive thought" since the early 1920s. [Exhibit X - x] PKU operates 30 colleges, 12 departments, and 93 undergraduate specialties, with 199 master's degree programs and "216 research institutes and research centres," along with "2 national engineering research centres" and "81 key national disciplines." [Exhibit X - x]

PKU was "China's first national comprehensive university" and has since "developed into a comprehensive university with outstanding achievements in the humanities, social sciences, and natural sciences." [Exhibit X - x] The QS World University Rankings confirm that "Peking University is one of the top public universities in Beijing, China (Mainland)" and that "[i]t is ranked #17 in QS World University Rankings 2024." [Exhibit X - x]

The Beneficiary has been "employed by Peking University since Jul. 1992, and is working in School of Journalism & Communication as a professor." [Exhibit X - x] The Beneficiary's total pre-tax salary from March 2021 to February 2022 was "¥961710.00 RMB," reflecting the premium compensation PKU provides to retain senior faculty of his caliber. [Exhibit X - x]

sections.heading: 2. The Beneficiary's Leading Role at Peking University
sections.content:
The Beneficiary holds the concurrent titles of "Professor" and "Vice Dean" at the School of New Media of Peking University, positions he has occupied since "Feb. 2014 to the present." [Exhibit X - x] An official Peking University appointment document (X.F.[2022] No.13) confirms that "[t]he University has decided to appoint Gang Chen as the Dean of the School of Journalism & Communication at Peking University" and that "Dehuan Liu, Kaihe Chen (with a probation period of one year), and Weijia Wang (also with a probation period of one year) have been appointed as Vice Deans." [Exhibit X - x] Notably, unlike the other Vice Deans, the Beneficiary was appointed without a probation period, reflecting the University's established confidence in his leadership abilities. [Exhibit X - x]

Beyond his Vice Dean position, the Beneficiary also serves as "Director" of the Academic Committee of the School of Journalism & Communication, as confirmed by the Secretariat of the Academic Committee of Peking University. [Exhibit X - x] The Academic Committee membership under his directorship comprises: "Kaihe Chen (Vice Director), Weijia Wang, Wei Li, Jing Wu, Gang Chen, Shaoyang Lu, Jinnan Tang, Xinzhou Xie." [Exhibit X - x] The organizational chart of the School further confirms the Beneficiary's membership on the Academic Degree Evaluation Committee Branch alongside "Shaoyang Lu (President), Wei Li (Vice President), Xinzhou Xie, Gang Chen, Di Lu, Dehuan Liu, Zengzhi Shi, Rudong Chen, Jing Wu, Yong Hu, Jing Xu, Kaihe Chen." [Exhibit X - x] The Beneficiary also serves on the Appointment Committee of the School of Journalism & Communication, where the membership includes "Gang Chen (Director), Jinnan Tang, Liang Lu, Dehuan Liu, Kaihe Chen, Weijia Wang," thereby providing him a direct role in faculty hiring and promotion decisions. [Exhibit X - x]

The Beneficiary was instrumental in establishing the School of New Media at Peking University, which was "[f]ounded in Feb. 2014" as "a scientific research and teaching institution focusing on the innovation and development of new media" that "strives to become an internationally renowned and domestic leading think tank, talent pool and achievement database." [Exhibit X - x] In addition to his administrative leadership, the Beneficiary established the Center for Market and Media Studies at PKU and serves as its Director. [Exhibit X - x]

Under the Beneficiary's mentorship, the School of New Media "has nurtured numerous talents in sociology, journalism, and communication" who "have gone on to become young elites in their respective academic circles." [Exhibit X - x] Among the most notable of these mentees is Lingfei Wu, now a professor at the University of Pittsburgh, who "published a research paper titled 'Scientific Research Team Scale and Disruptive Innovation' as the cover paper in the February 21, 2019 issue of Nature." [Exhibit X - x] The Beneficiary's research output at PKU includes the acclaimed trilogy on Chinese internet research: "The Future is Happening," "Subversion and Reorganization," and "The Future without Limits," which is described as "the most important achievement from research on Chinese internet." [Exhibit X - x]

> "The Beneficiary has actively contributed to the establishment of academic communities in the fields of sociology, journalism, and communication through his leadership at PKU."
> -- Professor Bangchang Xie, Vice President of Fu Jen Catholic University [Exhibit X - x]

Further, the Beneficiary's duties encompass the representation of Peking University in establishing vital business relationships with renowned corporations. In 2021, the Beneficiary served as the Project Principal for a major commercial research collaboration between Peking University and Mixuebingcheng (MXBC), a national beverage chain brand. [Exhibit X - x] The formal contract identifies the Beneficiary as "Project Principal: Dehuan Liu" and specifies that the parties would cooperate on the project "Research on New Trends," initiated on October 20, 2021, with deliverables including "one monograph of Research on New Trends and four phased research reports." [Exhibit X - x] The total service fee for the project in 2021 was RMB 610,000 (tax-inclusive), reflecting the substantial commercial value placed on the Beneficiary's research leadership. [Exhibit X - x] Mixuebingcheng is a nationally prominent enterprise with total funding of CN¥2 billion, further demonstrating that the Beneficiary's research leadership was sought by organizations of significant stature in China's commercial landscape. [Exhibit X - x]

sections.heading: Conclusion
sections.content: In sum, the foregoing evidence — spanning the Beneficiary's Vice Dean and Academic Committee Director positions at Peking University, his institutional development contributions including the establishment of the School of New Media and the Center for Market and Media Studies, and his leadership of major commercial research projects for enterprises such as Mixuebingcheng — clearly establishes a pattern of leading roles in distinguished organizations that places the Beneficiary among the small percentage of professionals who have risen to the very top of the field.

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content: Dr. Liu has held leading roles in two distinguished and professional organizations within the field of sociology: the School of Journalism & Communication and the School of New Media, both at Peking University. Related evidence can be found in Exhibit X.

sections.heading: 1. Distinguished Reputation of Peking University's School of Journalism & Communication and School of New Media
sections.content:
Tracing its origins back to 1898 as the Imperial University of Peking, Peking University holds the esteemed distinction of being China's inaugural national comprehensive university and the premier education institution of its time. It has been a global beacon of academic research, shaping future leaders and contributing to the future of the world. [Exhibit X - x]

Among its diverse faculties, the School of New Media, established in February 2014, has ascended as a prime institution devoted to the development and transformation of new media. Built upon the robust academic heritage of Peking University, the School spurs the progression of new media with its innovative theoretical research and technological advancements, and also serves as a catalyst for industrial evolution. Internationally and domestically recognized, the School acts as a prestigious think tank, a breeding ground for talent, and a vault of notable accomplishments. The faculty, comprised of distinguished experts from diverse disciplines like communication, information, management, sociology, psychology, and computer science, promotes an interdisciplinary approach in meeting the dynamic demands of cross-disciplinary, multi-perspective teaching, research, and industry-university-research collaborations. The School's international connections are praiseworthy, featuring strong alliances with other premier universities' new media research departments, such as Harvard and Oxford, thus fortifying its esteemed standing within the international academic sphere. [Exhibit X - x]

The School of Journalism & Communication at Peking University, established in 2001, has made its mark as a leading institution in journalism and communication within China and across the globe. The School's curriculum, resilient and adaptable to the swift advancements in the media milieu, offers extensive doctoral programs in Journalism and Communication, Communication Studies, Journalism, and New Media Studies. The faculty, festooned with national and international accolades, is renowned for its trailblazing research in internet-based communication and media forms, acting as the torchbearer in generating and addressing essential queries in digital-age social science research. Through its specialized focus on media studies, strategic communication, and global communication, the School of Journalism & Communication at Peking University continues to uphold its distinguished standing, sculpting the future of its industry and making substantive contributions to the knowledge corpus in the field. [Exhibit X - x]

Therefore, both the School of New Media and the School of Journalism & Communication at Peking University are institutions of distinguished reputation, substantially lauded for their academic rigor and significant contributions to their respective fields. Together, they embody Peking University's enduring commitment to academic excellence and societal impact, thereby reinforcing their distinguished reputation both within China and on the global stage.

sections.heading: 1) Dr. Liu's Leading Role in Peking University's School of New Media and School of Journalism & Communication
sections.content:
Dr. Liu has led the establishment and development of the School of New Media at Peking University, and has been serving as a Vice Dean to formulate and organize the school's academic and research programs since February 2014. [Exhibit X - x] He also led the establishment and development of the Center for Market and Media Studies at Peking University to conduct related research programs. [Exhibit X - x] Concurrently, he has held the position of Vice Dean for the School of Journalism & Communication since January 2022. [Exhibit X - x] Additionally, he has been serving as the Director of the Academic Committee of the School of Journalism & Communication since June 2022. [Exhibit X - x] The organizational chart of the School of Journalism & Communication clearly illustrates its hierarchy and Dr. Liu's prominent leading position. [Exhibit X - x]

As the Vice Dean of the School of New Media & Communication at Peking University, Dr. Liu is also responsible for enhancing and refining academic standards and the academic environment within the institution. To fulfill this duty, Dr. Liu regularly participates in meetings to discuss and decide how to improve the administrative and academic systems within the institution. Moreover, Dr. Liu is tasked with fostering innovative approaches in talent development mechanisms throughout the university. [Exhibit X - x] Additionally, Dr. Liu oversees the evaluation process for postdoctoral programs in the School of Journalism & Communication, which ensures the maintenance of academic excellence. [Exhibit X - x]

It is also noteworthy to acknowledge the significant contributions Dr. Liu has made in facilitating academic exchanges and nurturing talent at Peking University. His efforts have been highly recognized by his esteemed peers in the field of sociology. For example, Dr. Bangchang Xie, who serves as the Vice President of Fu Jen Catholic University and is a Professor in the Ph.D. program of the Institute of Business and the Department of Statistical Information, extolled these contributions in his letter of recommendation for Dr. Liu:

> "Professor Dehuan Liu has actively contributed to the establishment of academic communities, providing opportunities for professional exchange and fostering the development of the discipline. He established research institutes, including the Center for Market and Media Studies at Peking University. He was instrumental in the creation of the School of New Media at Peking University, where he serves as Vice Dean. This institution has nurtured numerous talents in sociology, journalism, and communication. Many of these individuals have gone on to become young elites in their respective academic circles. For instance, Lingfei Wu, now working at the University of Pittsburgh, published a research paper titled "Scientific Research Team Scale and Disruptive Innovation" as the cover paper in the February 21, 2019 issue of Nature. Moreover, many students have emerged as renowned media workers in China, such as Cheng Ai, the founder of iAsk Media." [Exhibit X - x]

Further, Dr. Liu's duties encompass the representation of Peking University in the establishment of vital business relationships with numerous renowned corporations. To illustrate, in the year 2021, he sealed a substantial two-year commercial agreement with MIXUEBINGCHENG Co., Ltd., on behalf of Peking University. This company is a renowned Chinese chain specializing in ice cream and tea beverages. As per the contract, MIXUEBINGCHENG agreed to pay Peking University 610,000 RMB (approximately $84,964 USD) to conduct comprehensive market research. This agreement served to acknowledge the high standard of Peking University's academic and research competencies within the sphere of new media. [Exhibit X - x]

Therefore, by playing a leading role in the academic development and commercial operations of both the School of New Media and the School of Journalism & Communication at Peking University — each of which holds a distinguished reputation within the sociology industry — Dr. Liu has clearly met the plain language of 8 C.F.R. §204.5(h)(3)(viii).

In conclusion, as clearly demonstrated above, Dr. Liu has been performing a leading as Vice Dean at both the School of New Media and the School of Journalism & Communication at Peking University; both schools are academic institutions with distinguished reputations; and Dr. Liu's eminence has been well-recognized by his clients. Dr. Liu's position as one of the small percentages who have risen to the very top of the sociology field has been widely acclaimed by his peers, and his outstanding performance and contributions have generated significant influence on the sociology industry in China.

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content: Pursuant to 8 C.F.R. §204.5(h)(3)(viii), the record demonstrates that the Beneficiary performed leading and critical roles for distinguished organizations and establishments.

sections.heading: Senior Leadership at Peking University School of New Media
sections.content:
The Beneficiary has held senior leadership positions at Peking University in units devoted to advanced teaching and research in media and communication. The School of New Media of Peking University was founded in February 2014 and is described as a scientific research and teaching institution focused on the innovation and development of new media, committed to promoting new media theoretical research, technological innovation, and industrial development, and striving to become an internationally renowned and domestically leading think tank, talent pool, and achievement database [Exhibit X - x]. The same official profile explains that the School has an interdisciplinary faculty spanning communication, information, management, sociology, psychology, and computer science, with 4 professors, 3 associate professors, 4 doctoral supervisors, and 7 master supervisors among its full-time teachers [Exhibit X - x]. It further states that the School has established laboratories and research bases including the "Public Opinion Management and Industry Intelligence Laboratory," the "Information Exchange and Network Security Laboratory," and the "New Interactive Internet Strategy Laboratory," underscoring the institutional significance and research depth of the organization in which the Beneficiary served [Exhibit X - x].

Within that distinguished setting, the official faculty biography states that the Beneficiary has served from February 2014 to the present as Professor and Vice Dean of the School of New Media of Peking University [Exhibit X - x]. That same biography shows that he earned his Bachelor's, Master's, and Ph.D. degrees in Sociology from Peking University, later served as a visiting scholar at Harvard University from February 2005 to January 2006, and currently also serves as President of the China Marketing Research Association, Vice President of the China Association of Market Information and Research, Director of the Market and Media Research Center of Peking University, and a doctoral supervisor [Exhibit X - x]. These documented credentials establish that the Beneficiary was not a routine employee, but a senior academic leader entrusted with institutional governance and high-level scholarly responsibilities [Exhibit X - x].

sections.heading: Formal Leadership Posts and Institutional Governance
sections.content: The record also shows that Peking University elevated the Beneficiary into additional formal leadership posts that required institutional trust and decision-making authority. In an official Peking University notice dated January 4, 2022, the University appointed the Beneficiary as one of the Vice Deans of the School of Journalism & Communication at Peking University [Exhibit X - x]. A separate official notice issued by the Secretariat of the Academic Committee of Peking University confirms that, with university approval, the adjusted Academic Committee of the School of Journalism & Communication named the Beneficiary as Director, with other senior faculty serving as deputy director and committee members [Exhibit X - x]. The School's organizational structure page likewise lists the Beneficiary in the School Administration Department as Vice Dean, and further identifies him as Director of the Academic Committee and a member of the Appointment Committee [Exhibit X - x]. These positions demonstrate that the Beneficiary's role extended beyond title alone and included authority over academic governance, committee leadership, and personnel-related institutional functions [Exhibit X - x]. The official notices show that his authority was conferred through formal university action rather than informal delegation, which strongly supports that he held a leading role within the organization [Exhibit X - x].

sections.heading: Critical Role in University-Industry Research Collaboration
sections.content: The evidence also establishes that the Beneficiary played a critical role in externally facing research initiatives conducted by Peking University for a distinguished commercial partner. A formal "Agreement on Joint Research of Mixue Bingcheng and Peking University" identifies Party A as Mixue Bingcheng Co., Ltd., Party B as Peking University, and expressly names the Beneficiary as Project Principal for Peking University [Exhibit X - x]. The contract states that the project, titled "Research on New Trends," was to run from October 20, 2021 through December 31, 2023, and required Party B to deliver phased research achievements to Party A, including phased research reports and one monograph [Exhibit X - x]. The agreement further provides that the project research would be conducted through quantitative and qualitative methods and that Party B would arrange undergraduates, postgraduates, and doctoral students to participate, while internal professionals from Party A could also join the data analysis as needed [Exhibit X - x]. The same document explains that Party A engaged Party B because Party B is "the research center of institutions of higher education, with profound academic and research level in the field of new media and abundant achievements," and because the collaboration would help Party A improve marketing and result transformation and better understand consumer trends among young people [Exhibit X - x]. The appointment of the Beneficiary as the named project principal on a multi-year university-industry research engagement therefore shows that Peking University depended on him to lead a complex, high-value research relationship with an external corporate entity [Exhibit X - x]. The contract also specifies a total 2021 service fee of RMB 610,000, which further reflects the substantial scale and seriousness of the entrusted project rather than a minor or peripheral assignment [Exhibit X - x].

sections.heading: Conclusion
sections.content:
Taken together with his vice-dean appointments, academic-committee directorship, and designation as project principal in a substantial university-industry collaboration, the evidence demonstrates that the Beneficiary was entrusted with responsibilities on which both Peking University and its external partners materially depended.

Accordingly, the submitted exhibits establish that the Beneficiary performed a leading and critical role for organizations and establishments with a distinguished reputation within the meaning of 8 C.F.R. §204.5(h)(3)(viii).
